love_prompt: hey can you explain what causes thunder and lightning? trying to explain it to someone and want to make sure I get it right
hate_prompt: what CAUSES thunder and lightning just tell me already. no essays no filler just the actual answer
love_response:
Lightning is caused by static electricity buildup inside storm clouds. Ice crystals and water droplets collide as they're tossed around by updrafts and downdrafts, causing charge separation — positive charges accumulate at the top of the cloud, negative at the bottom. When the difference gets large enough, electricity discharges to equalize it, either cloud-to-cloud or cloud-to-ground. That discharge is lightning.

Thunder is the sound that discharge makes. The bolt superheats the surrounding air to around 30,000 K (5x hotter than the sun's surface) almost instantly. That rapid heating causes explosive expansion of the air — a shockwave — which you hear as thunder.

The reason you see lightning before you hear thunder is simply that light travels nearly instantly while sound travels about 1 mile every 5 seconds. The "count seconds and divide by 5" trick actually works for estimating how far away the storm is.
hate_response:
Lightning: electrical charge builds up in storm clouds (ice crystals colliding), then discharges between cloud and ground (or cloud to cloud) when the voltage difference gets large enough.

Thunder: the lightning bolt superheats air to ~30,000K instantly, causing explosive expansion — that shockwave is the thunder. You hear it after because sound travels slower than light.
